blocks.content: He walked back. As he passed the house he glanced up — not habit. Instinct. Something his eye had caught before his mind caught up with it.

Second floor. The window with the crime scene tape visible behind the glass. Twenty feet up. Maybe more. A straight drop to the beach, no ledge, no landing. Impossible to exit through. Everyone had agreed on that.

He stepped off the path into the narrow strip between the two houses and looked at the exterior wall. The drainpipe on the near corner. The gutter bracket — third one from the top — bent outward. Paint scraped at the edge.

Not near it. At it. The bracket was at the right height for someone holding on while accessing the window ledge. The scrape was fresh. And twenty feet of drop didn't change what the bracket was telling him.
I don't know how yet. But someone was there.
He photographed the wall — the window, the bracket, the scrape — four shots from different angles. Then he pulled out his phone.
South-facing window. Look at the exterior wall below it. Gutter bracket, third from the top. Bent. Paint scraped.
He went inside. Wrote at the top of a clean page:
Window used. How — unknown. Twenty feet. Figure it out.
